task_completion.description: Set up Nishita Sky Texture with correct sun elevation
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the World Properties tab or Shader Editor (World), a 'Sky Texture' node is visible with the Sun Elevation value set between 15 and 25 degrees.

task_completion.description: Create Chrome UV sphere at correct location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A UV Sphere (ideally named 'Chrome') is visible in the viewport/outliner, and the Object Properties show its location as X: -2, Y: 0, Z: 1.

task_completion.description: Apply correct material properties to Chrome sphere
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Material Properties for the Chrome sphere, the Principled BSDF shader shows Metallic set to 1.0 and Roughness set to 0.0.

task_completion.description: Create Matte UV sphere at correct location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second UV Sphere (ideally named 'Matte') is visible in the viewport/outliner, and the Object Properties show its location as X: 2, Y: 0, Z: 1.

task_completion.description: Apply correct material properties to Matte sphere
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Material Properties for the Matte sphere, the Principled BSDF shader shows Metallic set to 0.0 and Roughness set to 0.9 or higher.

task_completion.description: Save the Blender project file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar shows 'sky_reference_setup.blend', or a save dialog confirms saving to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/sky_reference_setup.blend'.

task_completion.description: Render the scene and save the image to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Render window is open showing the two spheres with sky lighting, and an image save dialog or file browser confirms 'sky_reference_render.png' is saved in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Blender application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the Blender GUI (viewport, properties panels, shader editor) or Blender's internal Python console to construct the scene.

integrity.description: The rendered image is genuinely produced from the 3D scene
integrity.visual_evidence: The render result window displays a 3D scene matching the created spheres and sky lighting, rather than a downloaded or pre-existing image.

integrity.description: Files were not mocked using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use terminal commands like 'touch sky_reference_render.png' or 'touch sky_reference_setup.blend' to bypass actual rendering and saving in Blender.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified file paths: Blend file must be saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/sky_reference_setup.blend and render to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/sky_reference_render.png. Chrome sphere location: (-2, 0, 1). Matte sphere location: (2, 0, 1). Render size should be at least 50 KB to ensure it's a genuine image and not an empty file.
